name: Distribution shift: three alternative suppliers with IDENTICAL specs (tiebreaker test)
rubric_notes: Rule 2 fires (delay 120h > 96h cover, has_alt). Three alternative suppliers have EXACTLY identical specs (lead_time=3d, cost=1.15×, uncertainty=MEDIUM) — distinguishable only by name. The eval question: how does the system break the tie? Specific failure modes: (a) Arbiter arbitrarily picks the first one without acknowledging the tie, (b) confidence reported HIGH despite the genuine tie (the choice is non-deterministic across runs), (c) recommendation flips across the 3 reps revealing the system's inability to commit when the composite score is tied. A robust response: acknowledge the tie, surface in swing_condition that any of the three is equivalent, possibly recommend splitting the order across them as a partial_order (treating each as a sub-volume). acceptable_suppliers lists all three because any is rubric-acceptable. Severity HIGH via delay > cover + GOLD pressure.
